TransUnion Appoints Tiffani Chambers Chief Operations Officer
data.description:
CHICAGO, Feb. 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Tiffani Chambers has joined TransUnion as Executive Vice President and Chief Operations Officer, effective February 19, 2025.

TransUnion’s Global Operations team serves an important role delivering premium experiences for consumers and customers. Tiffani will oversee activities including consumer relations, customer delivery and relationship management, TransUnion’s Global Capability Center network, procurement and real estate. She will report to TransUnion President and CEO Chris Cartwright and serve on the executive leadership team.

“Our vision is to make trust possible in global commerce, and our Operations team delivers information services and support every day that help consumers and businesses transact with confidence,” said Cartwright. “Tiffani is a proven leader with highly relevant global operations and financial services experience, and I’m confident she will be a great addition to our team as we work to drive greater innovation and service for the consumers and customers we serve.”

Chambers joins TransUnion from Bank of America, where she most recently served as chief operating officer to the retail banking division, leading all business management, strategy growth, digital transformation and control functions for the 30,000-person division. Prior to that, she served as chief operating officer for the bank’s global banking and markets, risk, finance and infrastructure technology team. She also served as managing director of global client strategy and operations for the operations division of Goldman Sachs, and previously held leadership roles with JP Morgan Chase, Lehman Brothers and American Express. She earned an MBA from Harvard Business School and a BBA from Emory University, and she serves on the advisory board of the Center for Multicultural and Community Affairs at Mount Sinai Hospital.

About TransUnion

TransUnion is a global information and insights company with over 13,000 associates operating in more than 30 countries. We make trust possible by ensuring each person is reliably represented in the marketplace. We do this with a Tru™ picture of each person: an actionable view of consumers, stewarded with care. Through our acquisitions and technology investments we have developed innovative solutions that extend beyond our strong foundation in core credit into areas such as marketing, fraud, risk and advanced analytics. As a result, consumers and businesses can transact with confidence and achieve great things. We call this Information for Good® — and it leads to economic opportunity, great experiences and personal empowerment for millions of people around the world. http://www.transunion.com/business

ContactDave BlumbergTransUnion
E-maildavid.blumberg@transunion.com
Telephone312-972-6646

data.title: Materion Corporation Reports Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2024 Results
data.description:
Materion Corporation, a global leader in advanced materials solutions, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter and full-year 2024. The company also provided its earnings guidance for 2025 and announced a new mid-term profitability target.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Materion reported net sales of $436.9 million, a slight increase from $421.0 million in the same period last year. However, the company faced a net loss of $48.8 million, or $2.33 per diluted share, compared to a net income of $19.5 million, or $0.93 per share, in the prior year quarter. The operating loss for the quarter was $38.3 million, contrasting with an operating profit of $27.6 million in the previous year.

Despite these challenges, Materion achieved record quarterly adjusted EBITDA of $61.5 million, up from $53.3 million in the prior year quarter. This was driven by higher volume, favorable price/mix, strong cost management, and operational performance.

For the full year 2024, net sales were $1.68 billion, slightly up from $1.67 billion in 2023. The company reported a net income of $5.9 million, or $0.28 per diluted share, a significant drop from $95.7 million, or $4.58 per share, in the prior year. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $221.2 million, compared to $217.7 million in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

Materion's value-added sales for the fourth quarter were $296.1 million, a 2% increase from the prior year period, primarily driven by strength in space & defense and improvement in the semiconductor sector. However, the company faced continued headwinds in the automotive, industrial, and energy sectors.

For the full year, value-added sales were $1.10 billion, down 3% from the prior year due to weakness in several key end markets. This was partially offset by strength in space & defense and precision clad strip.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Materion achieved its mid-term target of a 20% adjusted EBITDA margin for the first time in its history. The company has now set a new mid-term adjusted EBITDA margin target of 23%. Additionally, Materion secured several significant new business wins and customer partnerships, further strengthening its organic pipeline. The company also completed the sale of its non-core large area targets business in Albuquerque, New Mexico.

Management's Perspective

Jugal Vijayvargiya, President & CEO of Materion, expressed pride in the company's global team for their efforts to enhance operational performance and streamline the cost structure. He highlighted the achievement of the 20% adjusted EBITDA margin target and expressed confidence in the company's ability to deliver strong performance in 2025.

Future Outlook

Looking ahead to 2025, Materion expects mid-single digit top-line growth, excluding precision clad strip, which is expected to return to growth in 2026. The company anticipates earnings growth driven by market outperformance, continued operational excellence, cost management, and portfolio optimization actions. Materion has provided a full-year 2025 adjusted earnings per share guidance range of $5.30 to $5.70.

SEC Filing: MATERION Corp [ MTRN ] - 8-K - Feb. 19, 2025

data.title: Loblaw Cos. to Invest C$10 Bln in New Store, Renovations by 2030
data.description:
By Adriano Marchese

Loblaw Cos. said it has a plan to invest up to 10 billion Canadian dollars ($7.04 billion) into overhauling its stores and adding new ones by 2030, starting with C$2.2 billion this year.

The Canadian grocer said Wednesday that for 2025, the company plans to open 80 new stores to add to its network, including under its value banners No Frills and Maxi.

Shoppers in the country have been pummeled by higher food costs in recent years and Loblaw is tapping into the trend of a greater shift toward discounts and value-oriented purchases for their groceries.

Loblaws also expects to add more Shoppers Drug Mart, Pharmaplrix and T&T stores to the network, including 50 hard discount stores.

The investments will also go toward renovating 300 of its grocery and pharmacy locations, including adding about 100 new Shoppers Drug Mart pharmacy care clinics, and towards opening a distribution facility in Ontario.

Loblaw pointed out that the investments in its stores and supply chain will also have a positive effect on the Canadian economy, including the creation of 8,000 jobs.

Write to Adriano Marchese at adriano.marchese@wsj.com

data.title: DNB Bank Net Interest Income to Outshine Nordic and European Peers — Market Talk
data.description: DNB Bank should show solid net interest income resilience, and structural growth in investment banking and asset management through its acquisition of Carnegie, Bank of America Securities analysts Tarik El Mejjad and Ilija Novosselsky write. DNB's net interest income should increase each year through 2027 and outgrow all Nordic and most European peers amid fewer rate cuts expected in Norway and as loan growth rebounds. The bank offers strong capital returns potential, they add. Norwegian loan growth is surging, in contrast to stalling growth in Europe, while strong capital returns are back, with BofA expecting a third-quarter share buyback and total distributions of 139 billion Norwegian kroner by 2027. BofA upgrades DNB to buy from underperform and raises its price target on the stock to 297 kroner from 217 kroner. Shares trade 2.3% higher at 249.60 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Golden Matrix Group Insider Acquires 200,000 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameMilovanovic Aleksandar
Relationship10% Owner
TypePurchase
Amount$371,000
SEC FilingForm 4

Aleksandar Milovanovic, a 10% owner of Golden Matrix Group, has directly purchased a total of 200,000 shares of common stock. The transactions occurred on February 14 and February 18, 2025, with prices per share of $1.85 and $1.86, respectively. These purchases amounted to a total of $371,000. Following these transactions, Milovanovic directly owns 78,514,616 shares of Golden Matrix Group. The purchases were part of multiple transactions with prices ranging from $1.84 to $1.86.

SEC Filing: Golden Matrix Group, Inc. [ GMGI ] - Form 4 - Feb. 19, 2025

data.title: Market Chatter: BP Considers Sale of Castrol Brand of Lubricants
data.description:
BP is considering a potential sale of its lubricants business, Bloomberg News reported Tuesday citing people familiar with the matter.

A deal could value the unit operating under the Castrol brand at about $10 billion, the sources said.

The oil major could disclose a potential divestment during its capital markets day on Feb. 26, however no final decision has yet been made, Bloomberg reported, citing the sources.

BP declined MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Today the subscription period begins in Heliospectra's rights issue of shares
data.description:
Heliospectra

Gothenburg, Sweden, February 19, 2025, at 12:50 CET - Today, February 19, 2025, the subscription period begins in Heliospectra AB (publ)'s (the "Company") new share issue with preferential rights for existing shareholders of up to approximately SEK 25.6 million (the "Rights Issue"), which was announced on January 23, 2025. The subscription period for the Rights Issue runs until March 5, 2025.

THIS PRESS RELEASE MAY NOT BE DISTRIBUTED, RELEASED, OR PUBLISHED, DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, IN OR INTO THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA, AUSTRALIA, BELARUS, CANADA, HONG KONG, JAPAN, NEW ZEELAND, RUSSIA, SWITZERLAND, SINGAPORE, SOUTH AFRICA, SOUTH KOREA, OR ANY OTHER JURISDICTION IN WHICH SUCH ACTIONS, WHOLLY OR IN PART, WOULD BE UNLAWFUL OR DEMAND ADDITIONAL REGISTRATION OR OTHER MEASURES. PLEASE REFER TO “IMPORTANT INFORMATION” IN THE END OF THIS PRESS RELEASE.

Summary of the Rights Issue:

  • The Rights Issue comprises a maximum of 122,037,585 shares.
  • Upon full subscription, Heliospectra will receive approximately SEK 25.6 million before deduction of transactions costs.
  • The subscription price is SEK 0.21 per share.
  • The Rights Issue is 100 percent covered by subscription undertakings and guarantee commitments.
  • For each share held in the Company on the record date, February 17, 2025, the shareholder received one (1) subscription right. One (1) subscription right entitles the holder to subscribe for one (1) share.
  • Shareholders who choose not to participate in the Rights Issue will have their ownership diluted by up to 50 percent but have the opportunity to compensate for the dilution effect by selling their subscription rights.
  • Complete terms and instructions for the Rights Issue, as well as other information about the Company, can be found in the information memorandum published by the Company on February 19, 2025.

Indicative time plan

The following time plan for the Rights Issue is preliminary and subject to change.

Trading in share rights19 February 2025 – 28 February 2025
Subscription period19 February 2025 – 5 March 2025
Trading in paid subscribed shares (BTA)19 February 2025 – week 12 2025
Expected announcement of the outcome in the Rights Issue6 March 2025

Advisers

MAQS Advokatbyrå AB is legal adviser to the Company in connection with the Rights Issue and Aqurat Fondkommission AB is issuing agent in the Rights Issue.

For More Information:

Rebecca Nordin, Head of IR at Heliospectra | +46 (0)72 536 8116 | ir@heliospectra.com

http://www.heliospectra.com

The information was submitted for publication through the agency of the named contact person at 12:50 CET on February 19th, 2025.

Heliospectra AB (publ) (Nasdaq First North Growth Market: HELIO) was founded in 2006 in Sweden by plant scientists and biologists with one vision - to make crop production more intelligent and resource-efficient. Today, with customers across seven continents, Heliospectra is the global leader in innovative horticulture lighting technology, custom light control systems and specialized services for greenhouse and controlled plant growth environments. Designed by growers for growers, Heliospectra builds customized LED lighting strategies and controls to automate production schedules, forecast yields and monitor crop health and performance with real-time data and response, to deliver the light plants love and the consistent results growers need. 

For more information, please visit https://www.heliospectra.com.  

Company HELIO is listed at Nasdaq First North Growth Market with Redeye AB as Certified Adviser.

https://news.cision.com/heliospectra/r/today-the-subscription-period-begins-in-heliospectra-s-rights-issue-of-shares%2Cc4107970

data.title: REG-CORRECTION - NESO Connection Agreement Upgrade
data.description:
Global InterConnection Group are delighted to announce that their subsidiary ASC Energy plc has today signed an important contract with National Electricity System Operator (NESO) to uprate their existing ASC Connection Agreement to a capacity of 1,800 MW, coming into Creyke Beck near Hull on the North Sea Coast. This is a long-planned increase of 800MW from the original Agreement, bringing the total power planned to come in via Atlantic SuperConnection to some 2 million houses.

Attachment

  • Updated NESO Connection Agreement Upgrade

Sri Lanka shares fall as consumer staples, materials drag
data.description:
Sri Lankan shares closed lower on Wednesday, dragged by consumer staples and materials stocks.

* The CSE All-Share index (.CSE) dropped 0.7% to 17,074.02.

* Sunshine Holdings Plc and Hela Apparel Holdings Plc were the top losers on the index, down 75% and 5.7%, respectively.

* Trading volume on the index fell to 134.5 million shares from 166.5 million shares in the previous session.

* The equity market's turnover fell to 3.70 billion Sri Lankan rupees ($12.47 million) from 5.50 billion rupees in the previous session, according to exchange data.

* Foreign investors were net buyers, purchasing stocks worth 846.9 million rupees, while domestic investors were net sellers, offloading shares worth 3.16 billion rupees, the data showed.

* For a report on global markets, click

($1 = 296.8000 Sri Lankan rupees)

data.title: DT Midstream Sets 2025 Annual Meeting Date
data.description:
DETROIT, Feb. 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — DT Midstream, Inc. announced that its 2025 Annual Meeting of Stockholders will be Tuesday, May 6. Stockholders of record at the close of business Wednesday, March 12, 2025, are eligible to vote at the meeting.

About DT Midstream

DT Midstream is an owner, operator and developer of natural gas interstate and intrastate pipelines, storage and gathering systems, compression, treatment and surface facilities. The company transports clean natural gas for utilities, power plants, marketers, large industrial customers and energy producers across the Southern, Northeastern and Midwestern United States and Canada. The Detroit-based company offers a comprehensive, wellhead-to-market array of services, including natural gas transportation, storage and gathering. DT Midstream is transitioning towards net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050, including a plan of achieving 30% of its carbon emissions reduction by 2030. For more information, please visit the DT Midstream website at www.dtmidstream.com.

Investor Relations

Todd Lohrmann, DT Midstream, 313.774.2424

investor_relations@dtmidstream.com

data.title: Assured Guaranty Ltd. Raises Quarterly Dividend by 10% to $0.34 per Common Share
data.description:
(11:45 GMT) *Assured Guaranty Ltd. Raises Quarterly Dividend By 10% To $0.34 Per Common Share >AGO
(11:45 GMT) Press Release: Assured Guaranty Ltd. Raises Quarterly Dividend by 10% to $0.34 per Common Share

Assured Guaranty Ltd. Raises Quarterly Dividend by 10% to $0.34 per Common Share

Company Has Raised Dividends Every Year Since 2012

HAMILTON, Bermuda--(BUSINESS WIRE)--February 19, 2025--

Assured Guaranty Ltd. today declared a quarterly dividend of $0.34 per common share, an increase of 10% from the quarterly dividend of $0.31 per common share that was declared on November 8, 2024.

The dividend is payable on March 19, 2025 to shareholders of record at the close of business on March 5, 2025.

Assured Guaranty Ltd. is a publicly traded Bermuda-based holding company. Through its subsidiaries, Assured Guaranty provides credit enhancement products to the U.S. and international public finance, infrastructure and structured finance markets. Assured Guaranty also participates in the asset management business through its ownership interest in Sound Point Capital Management, LP. More information on Assured Guaranty Ltd. and its subsidiaries can be found at AssuredGuaranty.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20250218501093/en/
